fix: replace unsafe ExecException cast with type guard in execute()

The catch block was using `err as ExecException` — an unsafe cast that
bypasses TypeScript's unknown-type narrowing. Replace with a structural
type guard:

  const execErr =
    typeof err === 'object' && err !== null && 'code' in err
      ? (err as ExecException)
      : null;

Then access all ExecException fields (code, stdout, stderr, signal, killed)
via optional chaining with the same `typeof` guards that were already in
place, falling back to safe defaults when execErr is null.

In practice exec() always rejects with ExecException on command failure, but
the null branch makes the code safe for any unexpected error shape.

Fixes 🔴 Critical finding from TypeScript review (step_19 workflow log).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix scaled KGP image shifting during occlusion

Integer division truncation in clip coordinate mapping caused adjacent
fragments to not tile perfectly. E.g., 128px in 30 cells: a fragment
at offset 10 got clipW=floor(5*128/30)=21, but the next fragment at
offset 15 started at clipX=floor(15*128/30)=64, leaving a 1px gap
(21+42=63 vs 64).

Fix: derive clipW from endpoint differences (clipEndX - clipX) instead
of computing width independently. This ensures adjacent fragments abut
exactly with no gaps or overlaps.

equals the full surface bounds — behavior unchanged. For transparent
nodes (BackdropNode), only the actual content area (e.g. the menu
popup) is registered as an occluder.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP occlusion layer mismatch for nested compositing

The occluder layer was saved from _kgpLayer (context-local) instead of
_kgpRegistry.CurrentLayer (global). When children rendered via path 2
push additional layers on the shared registry, _kgpLayer falls behind
registry._currentLayer. This caused menu popup occluders to be at the
same layer as the background KGP image (no occlusion) instead of a
higher layer (proper slicing).

Fix: save registry.CurrentLayer right after PushKgpLayer for the
deferred occluder registration. This ensures the occluder layer
accurately reflects the global ordering.

Updated test to verify the image is actually sliced (more placements
after menu open than before).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP window disappearing after drag + terminal resize

On resize, \x1b[2J clears text cells but KGP placements are terminal-level
state that persists until explicitly deleted. After dragging a KGP-containing
window, old placements at stale positions remained visible and interfered
with the re-rendered layout.

Fix: send KGP delete-all (\x1b_Ga=d,d=a,q=2\x1b\) before screen clear on
resize. Added HasEverTransmitted flag to KgpPlacementTracker that survives
Reset() to correctly detect when cleanup is needed.

Added two new integration tests:
- KGP window drag+resize survival (with KGP image)
- Non-KGP window drag+resize control test

commit.message:
Agent: Overengineered Block Cleanup (#7688)

* Agent: Overengineered Block Cleanup

* Initial plan

* Refactor CommentTracker.takeBetween from two-pass to single-pass partition

The original takeBetween() used a two-pass pattern: first collecting matching
array indices into an intermediate `indicesToRemove` array, then looping
backward over that array to splice entries out one by one (to avoid index
shifts as elements were removed).

This is the classic "collect indices then splice in reverse" anti-pattern that
obscures the intent behind unnecessary ceremony. It requires the reader to
track two separate loops and understand why the reverse order matters.

The new implementation replaces both loops with a single partition pass:
- `remaining` accumulates entries to keep (before range, failed predicate, after range)
- `results` accumulates comments to return (matching entries in the range)
- A single assignment `this.entries = remaining` replaces the reverse-splice

The logic is now directional and immediately readable: walk the sorted entries,
decide keep-or-extract for each one, done in one pass.

Also adds src/lint/test/rules/comment-tracker.test.ts with 12 unit tests
covering takeBetween boundary semantics, predicate filtering, state mutation,
and the checkpoint/rollback/commit lifecycle, which previously lacked dedicated
unit tests.

docs: update ADR-001 with Foundry agent client-side tools findings (#6)

Document findings from testing a Foundry-hosted agent with client-side
function tools (foundry_agent.py):

- Client-side function tools on Foundry agents work — the Agent Framework
  registers definitions, intercepts calls locally, and submits results
- Server-side MCP tool events (e.g. Foundry IQ) leak through the AG-UI
  stream, causing phantom tool_call_end errors that could not be resolved
- This is the primary reason the project uses Option 3 (Foundry agent as
  tool) rather than a Foundry-hosted orchestrator
- Added 'what would unblock' criteria for future re-evaluation

- Add missing dependencies to root package.json (@opencode-ai/plugin,
  @opencode-ai/sdk, @ai-eng-system/core)
- Fix build.ts to properly handle TEST_ROOT environment variable,
  skipping sync operations that access paths outside the test sandbox
- Fix loadConfig.ts to read TEST_ROOT at call time via getRoot() function
  instead of module load time (both src/ and packages/cli/src/)
- Fix task executor timeout using process groups (detached: true +
  process.kill(-pid)) for reliable child process termination
- Fix integration tests hardcoded path (/home/vitruvius/...) to use
  ORIGINAL_ROOT from process.cwd()
- Remove process.chdir() calls from integration and installation tests
  that caused test pollution during concurrent execution
- Fix build test error pattern assertions to match actual error messages
- Add TEST_ROOT isolation to config loading and CLI tests
- Increase task executor test timeouts for CI stability
- Clean up stale files: build.ts.tmp, .bak backup files
- Update .gitignore: add .ai-eng/runs/, .opencode/opencode.db*, *.bak

Test results improved from 769 pass / 84 fail to 794 pass / 59 fail
(25 fewer failures, ~30% reduction). Remaining failures are caused by
concurrent test pollution (shared process.env.HOME) and pass individually.

commit.message:
feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

* feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

Implements M2 (cryptd-7uw): the minimal slice that proves the full
L1 architecture is wired up and runnable end-to-end without LLM.

New packages:
- internal/engine: deterministic rules engine (NewGame, Move, Look)
  with typed errors NoExitError and LockedError
- internal/interpreter: RulesInterpreter maps text commands to
  EngineActions (go <dir>, look, quit and aliases)
- internal/narrator: TemplateNarrator produces fixed-template
  narrations for all event types
- internal/renderer: CLIRenderer reads stdin / writes stdout
- internal/game: Loop wires engine+interpreter+narrator+renderer;
  drives full pipeline until quit or context cancel

CLI:
- crypt headless --scenario <id>: runs headless mode; respects
  CRYPT_SCENARIO_DIR env var for scenario lookup path

Tests:
- Unit tests in all packages (no build tag)
- Integration tests in internal/game with adversarial fakes covering
  ctx cancel, channel close, quit event, narrate/render error paths
- E2E test (build tag e2e) compiles binary and runs scripted session
- Coverage ≥ 90% on all M2 packages (engine 93.8%, game 92.1%,
  narrator/renderer 100%, interpreter 95%)

Fixtures:
- testdata/scripts/minimal-run.yaml: M2 acceptance script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): address review — state corruption, quit narration, context, types

Addresses all issues surfaced by Cursor Bugbot review:

- engine: validate destination room before mutating GameState so a
  failed Move never leaves state corrupted (state-before-error-check)
- engine: hidden connections now return NoExitError (undiscoverable
  until revealed) instead of LockedError
